purpose: Auto-loaded (user-invocable: false) skill that defines the master's failure recovery protocol. Three sections: (1) Worker Return Validation table — STATUS/SUMMARY/FILES CHANGED/DIVERGENT ACTIONS validation rules, processing order; (2) On Failure Steps 0-7 — double failure check (repair tasks ending in `r` cannot spawn another repair), log failure, create repair task in initialization.json (the documented write-once exception, ID format `{wave}.{next}r`), rewire dependency chain, update chains[] if Chains type, dispatch repair worker with full original prompt + failed progress file + remediation guidance, log repair, eager dispatch scan; (3) Circuit Breaker — thresholds (3+ wave failures, 1 failure blocking 3+ downstreams, 1 failure blocking >50%), 7-step response (pause, gather, analyze, revision plan with categories modified/added/removed/retry, apply, log, resume). Permission gate: repair workers must report major deviations back to master, not proceed autonomously.
gotchas: Repair task IDs end in `r` — double failure detection depends on this convention
Repair task creation is the ONE documented exception to initialization.json's write-once rule (along with circuit breaker replan and !add_task)
Repair worker dispatch MUST include all 4 items: original prompt, failed progress file, repair task ID, instruction to follow failed_task.md
Circuit breaker pauses dispatches but the swarm 'continues around failures' — only direct dependents are blocked
Permission gate triggers dashboard popup via permission-level log entry
patterns: Numbered recovery steps (Step 0-7 for single failure; Step 1-7 for circuit breaker)
Auto-loaded protocol skill — referenced from master-protocol, p-track, p-track-resume
Categorized revision plan: modified | added | removed | retry
conventions: Step 0 is the gating check (double failure escalation)
